mapping.a_element: hypothetical Bernoulli treatment-assignment distribution whose spillover effect is the estimand
mapping.b_element: target policy whose value is being evaluated

mapping.a_element: actual randomized design / assignment mechanism that generated the observed data
mapping.b_element: behaviour policy that generated the offline logged data

mapping.a_element: Horvitz-Thompson estimator (inverse-probability / propensity weighting of observed outcomes)
mapping.b_element: vanilla importance sampling (unnormalized likelihood-ratio reweighting of logged rewards)
weighted importance sampling (self-normalized, bounded IS estimator)

mapping.a_element: exposure mapping / interference set defining each unit's relevant treatment configuration
mapping.b_element: context in the contextual bandit conditioning the reweighting

mapping.a_element: bias from a wrong interference set or incorrect exposure-mapping function

brief_justification: Both estimate the expected outcome under a hypothetical/target assignment distribution from data drawn under a different actual distribution via importance-sampling / inverse-probability weighting: paper A's Horvitz-Thompson and Hajek estimators are exactly (unnormalized) importance sampling and its self-normalized 'weighted importance sampling' variant that paper B uses for off-policy evaluation, and both must correct for/absorb bias when the assumed generating mechanism (interference set / exposure mapping vs. behaviour policy) is misspecified.
